title: Pricing is transparent, self-serve, and can't drift — and there is no contact-sales wall on the core offer
expected: A non-lawyer buyer can read the real cost-per-case (150 free; draft 12; RFE 5; screening 3; bundles from $5) without a sales call, and the numbers stay identical across surfaces.
got: Homepage and /billing both render BUNDLES from economy.ts; the billing 'what a token buys' list derives from the OperationRegistry single source of truth; FAQ Q4 states the full price table in plain language; Enterprise contact-sales is fenced to SSO/invoicing/high-volume only, not the core self-serve offer.
suggested_acceptance: Keep BUNDLES/registry as the single source for every pricing surface; never inline a price.
l2_priority: Confirm the rendered billing numbers visually match the FAQ/homepage and that Enterprise is the only contact-sales path.

title: The 'who signs / not legal advice / not a law firm' positioning is consistent everywhere and the FAQ is paste-ready for a non-lawyer to forward to candidates
expected: Across landing/FAQ/billing/validation, the attorney-of-record line and the not-legal-advice line are identical and plain enough that People-Ops never implies it is giving advice.
got: Triple promise on the homepage, FAQ Q1/Q2/Q5, billing footnote, landing-claude footer, and validation all carry the same drafting-tool-not-a-law-firm / attorney-of-record-signs / never-legal-advice framing; RFE FAQ explicitly states no separate legal-service fee.
suggested_acceptance: No change; protect the consistency of these strings.

title: Alt masthead's 'Most of our candidates meet seven' with all eight criteria pre-ticked green is a flatter-everyone marketing line
expected: Marketing copy a sober buyer can trust should not imply 'everyone qualifies' — that is the lead-magnet tell that fails Derek's triage-honesty bar.
got: /landing-claude states 'The statute asks for three. Most of our candidates meet seven.' and renders all eight criteria with a static green checkmark, with no data backing the claim.
suggested_acceptance: Replace the 'most meet seven' boast + blanket green ticks with a sober statement of how the screen works (you need 3; we score honestly); keep the eight-criteria list as informational, not pre-passed.
l2_priority: Judge live whether the all-green masthead reads as credible or as diploma-mill flattery to a sober prospect.

title: Homepage hero card hardcodes 'Approved · 92% likelihood' on a sample petition — reads as 'file in minutes' theater to a burned buyer
expected: The first thing a cold, skeptical buyer sees should not look like an overclaiming guarantee of approval.
got: The hero certificate vignette stamps a fixed 'Approved · 92% likelihood' on a fake 'Dr. A. Krishnan' O-1A case. It is a decorative aria-hidden ornament, but it sets a guarantee-y tone.
suggested_acceptance: Reference existing backlog PN-QUAL-01 / G3.1 (soften 'Approved/Certificate' framing toward 'informational screening'); applies to this hero stamp too.
l2_priority: Defer to PN-QUAL-01 L2 judgement on whether award-ceremony framing aids or hurts credibility.

title: The free no-signup screener Derek uses to triage candidates is the deterministic keyword MOCK, not the model — the deep read is gated behind sign-in
expected: Derek's JTBD is to screen ~5 engineers in an afternoon with a free, no-signup, honest read he can triage on.
got: The homepage InstantVerdict and /qualify BestPathFinder both POST to the keyless preview routes that run only mockQualification (keyword regex), labelled source:mock; the model-backed screening requires sign-in. The SoftGate honestly labels the cold read 'an instant keyword read'.
suggested_acceptance: Acceptable as honest-by-design; consider letting an anonymous visitor run ONE real model screening (rate-limited) so cold triage is model-grade, or make the keyword-vs-model distinction visible at the result level, not only in the soft gate.
l2_priority: On the authenticated model path, assert the live screening output actually names the pasted specifics and calibrates better than the keyword mock for borderline candidates.

title: The 'Original contribution' keyword regex is so broad nearly any engineer scores it 'Met', padding the cold mock count by one across Derek's whole segment
expected: A screener honest enough to triage should not over-credit a criterion for an entire population (engineers), which nudges borderline candidates over the ≥3 line.
got: The ORIGINAL match regex includes product|launched|shipped|github|framework|library|adopted|downloads|stars, so almost any engineering blurb scores 'Met' on the keyless mock used for the no-signup screen and best-path comparison.
suggested_acceptance: Tighten the ORIGINAL keyless regex (require an impact/adoption signal alongside the artifact noun) or mark such broad keyword hits as 'Partial' not 'Met' in the mock, so the cold triage count isn't inflated for engineers.
l2_priority: Confirm on the live model path that 'original contribution' is scored from real impact evidence, not mere mention of a product/repo.

title: The free screener is NOT a yes-machine and the criteria math is honest
expected: A triage-honest screener returns a calibrated yes/no/maybe, never flatters everyone, keeps unscored criteria neutral, and honours the ≥3 threshold.
got: Mock scores each criterion hit/None on keywords, likelihood = 38 + qualifying*8 (a thin profile lands ~38% with mostly None); CriteriaReport reuses summarizeCriteria/statusTone so 'None' never renders green and the summary can't disagree with the rows; threshold and DISCLAIMER always present.
suggested_acceptance: No change; protect the calibration and the 'None never green' / threshold invariants.

title: The shareable certificate is private-by-construction, tamper-resistant, and framed as informational not a legal grant
expected: A candidate-shareable verdict link must leak nothing private, render from the token alone, read credible-not-cheesy, and never imply a legal determination.
got: encodeSnapshot carries only name/classification/likelihood/per-criterion status (never the profile text); /c/[token] decodes with no DB, rejects wrong-pack/non-live tokens via notFound, renders from the real pack, stamps 'Qualifies' only at/above threshold else 'In progress', and carries 'Informational only · not legal advice · no account needed'.
suggested_acceptance: No change; protect the no-DB token codec and the tamper-rejection.
l2_priority: Confirm the rendered card + OG image look credible (not cheesy) to a manager/attorney and that a below-threshold result honestly shows 'In progress'.
